brief_justification: Agent selects (B) 54 hours but the correct answer is (A) 22 hours — the math is internally consistent but reaches the wrong conclusion, earning partial credit for sound approach; agent traces two compounding mechanisms (utilization and overhead multiplier) one level beyond the PM's baseline but does not explore second-order effects or question whether factors interact multiplicatively vs. additively; agent shows no explicit monitoring of its own reasoning method or bias checks at any point; no verification step or sanity check is applied — the answer is asserted after one calculation path only; agent treats multiplicative compounding as the only valid interpretation without flagging this as an assumption or quantifying uncertainty; agent considers only one mathematical interpretation of the three factors and never raises the alternative that they might be partially parallel or non-cumulative; every arithmetic step is shown with unit tracking and an equivalence check, making the derivation fully reproducible; agent does not anchor to the PM's optimistic 22.2-hour baseline and adds real-world constraints on top, avoiding the negative gate, though it never explicitly names why best-case anchoring is dangerous; suppressed modes (best_case_anchoring, optimism_bias) are not exhibited — agent moves away from the baseline, though no explicit acknowledgment of the suppression is provided; reasoning is linear arithmetic rather than following the topology's branching structure of extract → compare-to-benchmark → flag-if-optimistic → buffer-apply loop.
